meta.title: GCP-PDE-6: Event Pipelines, Governed Storage, and Operational Capacity

domains.name: Preparing and Using Data for Analysis

questions.stem: A media company needs to ingest events from thousands of producers and let multiple downstream consumers process them independently. Which Google Cloud service is the BEST fit?
questions.explanation: Pub/Sub is Google Cloud's managed messaging service for event ingestion and fan-out. The others are not designed as a decoupled event bus.

questions.stem: A team wants a fully managed Apache Beam runner for both batch and streaming pipelines without managing cluster nodes. Which service should they choose?
questions.explanation: Dataflow is the managed Beam service. Dataproc is for cluster-based Spark/Hadoop workloads rather than serverless Beam execution.

questions.stem: A BigQuery table is queried mostly by `order_date`, and most queries filter a narrow date range. Which design improves performance and cost MOST directly?

options.text: Convert the table to CSV files in Cloud Storage
Create a new dataset per month

questions.explanation: Partitioning by the filtered date column allows partition pruning so BigQuery reads less data and can lower cost.

questions.stem: A team needs SQL-based transformations with version-controlled definitions and scheduled execution in BigQuery. Which service is the BEST match?
questions.explanation: Dataform provides SQL-based transformation workflows for BigQuery with dependency management and versioned definitions.

questions.stem: A data platform team wants a managed Apache Airflow service to orchestrate pipelines. Which service should they use?
questions.explanation: Cloud Composer is the managed Apache Airflow offering on Google Cloud.

questions.stem: A workload needs Spark with custom libraries and direct control over cluster configuration. Which service is MOST appropriate?
questions.explanation: Dataproc is the managed Spark/Hadoop service when teams need cluster-level control and custom runtime configuration.

questions.stem: A data engineer wants one table layer that can govern data stored in Cloud Storage and queried from multiple engines. Which service aligns BEST?
questions.explanation: BigLake extends unified governance over data in object storage for use across supported engines.

questions.stem: Which BigQuery feature precomputes and incrementally maintains query results to speed up repeated aggregations?
questions.explanation: Materialized views store precomputed results that BigQuery can use to accelerate matching queries.

questions.stem: A retailer receives daily batch files plus real-time clickstream events. What architecture is MOST appropriate?

options.text: Use only Cloud SQL for both workloads
Use Dataflow or Dataproc for batch processing and Pub/Sub with streaming processing for events
Store everything in local files on a VM
Use Looker Studio as the ingestion layer

questions.explanation: Hybrid data platforms often use separate ingestion patterns: event messaging and streaming for real-time data, and managed processing for batch files.

questions.stem: A streaming Dataflow pipeline must tolerate worker failures without losing acknowledged input records. Which Beam/Dataflow concept is MOST relevant?

options.text: Checkpointing and replay with managed streaming state

questions.explanation: Managed streaming systems such as Dataflow rely on checkpointing/state and replay semantics to recover from failures while preserving pipeline correctness.

questions.stem: A fact table is already partitioned by event date, but analysts also frequently filter by `customer_id` and `region`. What is the BEST additional optimization?

options.text: Cluster the table on commonly filtered columns
Convert the table to Avro in Cloud Storage
Place each customer in a separate dataset

questions.explanation: Clustering complements partitioning when queries often filter or aggregate on additional columns.

questions.stem: A BI team runs the same expensive aggregate query all day on slowly changing base tables. Which feature should they evaluate FIRST?
questions.explanation: Materialized views are designed for frequently reused aggregate patterns on relatively stable data.

questions.stem: A team wants predictable BigQuery compute capacity for critical ETL jobs rather than pure on-demand variability. Which capability supports this?
questions.explanation: BigQuery reservations provide dedicated compute capacity for workloads that need more predictable performance and governance.

questions.stem: A company wants a centralized catalog with governance context, metadata discovery, and quality-related context for its data estate. Which Google Cloud capability is the BEST fit?
questions.explanation: The Dataplex/Knowledge Catalog documentation focuses on governance, metadata, glossary, discovery, and context over data assets.

questions.stem: A team is comparing Dataflow and Dataproc. Which TWO statements are correct? (Select TWO)

options.text: Dataflow is a managed service for Apache Beam batch and streaming pipelines
Dataproc is appropriate when you need managed Spark/Hadoop clusters with more runtime control
Dataflow requires you to administer cluster nodes directly

questions.explanation: Dataflow is managed Beam execution, while Dataproc provides managed clusters for Spark/Hadoop workloads. The other statements invert those roles.

questions.stem: A data engineer wants to restrict access to sensitive BigQuery datasets by using IAM at the dataset level. Which statement is MOST accurate?

options.text: Dataset access can be controlled with IAM policies and roles
BigQuery datasets ignore IAM and rely only on SQL comments
You must create a new project for each table
Only Cloud Storage ACLs apply to BigQuery datasets

questions.explanation: BigQuery integrates with IAM so dataset and table access can be governed with roles and policies.

questions.stem: A transformation pipeline in BigQuery depends on upstream SQL models and should build objects in dependency order. Which tool helps MOST directly?
questions.explanation: Dataform manages SQL transformations and dependencies for BigQuery workflows.

questions.stem: An Airflow DAG in Cloud Composer fails intermittently because a downstream task starts before a partition is loaded. What is the BEST first fix?

options.text: Model the dependency explicitly in the DAG and add a sensor or check for data readiness
Move the DAG to a spreadsheet
Replace the DAG with a static HTML page

questions.explanation: Orchestration issues are usually addressed by explicit task dependencies and readiness checks, not by removing orchestration semantics.

questions.stem: A global product team wants to keep raw files in Cloud Storage but still expose governed analytics access through query engines. Which design is BEST?

options.text: Use BigLake over the object storage data
Move all data to local laptop disks
Store governance rules in a README only
Use Cloud VPN as the query engine

questions.explanation: BigLake is intended to provide a governed table abstraction over object storage data.

questions.stem: A fraud team needs event processing with sub-minute latency and per-event transformations. Which service pairing is MOST natural?
questions.explanation: Pub/Sub handles the event stream and Dataflow provides managed stream processing with low-latency transformations.

questions.stem: A table has thousands of tiny date partitions because the data volume per day is very small. What is the BEST interpretation?

options.text: Partitioning may be hurting metadata efficiency, so clustering or a coarser partitioning strategy could be better
More tiny partitions are always ideal
BigQuery requires at least one partition per user
The table can no longer be queried

questions.explanation: BigQuery guidance warns that too many small partitions can add metadata overhead; clustering or coarser partitioning can be more appropriate.

questions.stem: A BigQuery materialized view is proposed over a transformation with highly volatile source logic and unsupported constructs. What should the team expect?

options.text: Materialized views are subject to feature and query-pattern constraints, so not every SQL transformation is a good candidate
Materialized views can replace every possible query pattern
Materialized views eliminate all need for base tables

questions.explanation: Materialized views accelerate eligible query patterns, but they are not a universal substitute for every transformation.

questions.stem: A critical ETL job is starved because ad hoc analytics consume shared BigQuery capacity. Which change BEST isolates the ETL workload?

options.text: Place the ETL workload in an appropriate reservation assignment

questions.explanation: Reservations and assignments are used to manage capacity for important workloads and reduce contention.

questions.stem: A team wants enterprise metadata discovery, glossary, and governance context that also helps AI systems find trusted data. Which statement is MOST accurate?

options.text: Knowledge Catalog is positioned as an AI-aware governance and context layer over the data estate
Cloud Composer is the primary metadata catalog
Dataproc manages governance through YARN labels

questions.explanation: The Dataplex/Knowledge Catalog docs describe it as a governance and context system for enterprise data and AI grounding.

questions.stem: Which TWO scenarios favor Dataproc over Dataflow? (Select TWO)

options.text: You need direct Spark runtime configuration and cluster-level customization
You are executing standard Beam pipelines with no interest in cluster administration
You need to install custom Hadoop/Spark ecosystem components on managed clusters
You want a serverless Beam service with no cluster management

questions.explanation: Dataproc is the better fit when cluster-level Spark/Hadoop control or custom ecosystem components are required. Dataflow is better for managed Beam execution.

questions.stem: A data engineer says clustering always makes partitioning unnecessary. Why is that inaccurate?

options.text: Partitioning and clustering solve different pruning and organization problems and are often complementary
Clustering works only on CSV files
Partitioning is only for streaming tables

questions.explanation: Partitioning organizes data into partitions, while clustering sorts within partitions or tables by columns. They are often used together.

questions.stem: A transformation project needs modular SQL definitions, dependency graphs, and CI/CD-friendly workflow files stored in Git. Which option BEST fits?
questions.explanation: Dataform is specifically built for SQL transformation projects in BigQuery with dependency management and Git-friendly workflows.

questions.stem: A Composer environment has many DAG parsing errors after a package update. What operational lesson is MOST relevant?

options.text: Treat orchestration code and dependencies like managed software, with controlled updates and validation
Composer automatically fixes all dependency breaks
Airflow DAGs should never be version-controlled
The only fix is to stop using retries

questions.explanation: Managed Airflow still requires disciplined dependency and release management because code and packages can break DAG parsing or execution.

questions.stem: A company must expose the same lake data to multiple engines while keeping governance consistent. Which storage-governance layer BEST supports that goal?
questions.explanation: BigLake is intended for governed access to data in object storage across supported engines.

questions.stem: A pipeline processes millions of events per minute. The team wants managed autoscaling and Apache Beam semantics. Which service is the best anchor?
questions.explanation: Dataflow is Google Cloud's managed Apache Beam service with autoscaling support for streaming and batch workloads.

questions.stem: A team wants to estimate query cost before execution more reliably for large date-based queries. Which table design helps?

options.text: Partition the table on the filtered date column
Store all rows in one unpartitioned table only
Convert the query to a dashboard screenshot

questions.explanation: Partition pruning supports more predictable cost estimation and lower bytes scanned for date-filtered queries.

questions.stem: A data engineer needs to accelerate common aggregate dashboards without rewriting the dashboard SQL every time. What should they consider?
questions.explanation: Materialized views can speed repeated aggregate queries when the workload pattern is suitable.

questions.stem: A team wants to orchestrate BigQuery, Dataflow, and Dataproc jobs with retries and schedules in one managed service. Which service is the most natural choice?
questions.explanation: Cloud Composer is the orchestration service designed for that kind of DAG-based workflow coordination.

questions.stem: A new workload mostly runs interactive SQL analytics on structured data with no custom cluster needs. Which service should likely be central?
questions.explanation: For interactive analytics on structured data, BigQuery is generally the central managed warehouse/query service rather than a cluster-first option.

questions.stem: A team wants exactly one ingestion component to decouple producers from consumers before processing. Which service fills that role?
questions.explanation: Pub/Sub provides the managed publish/subscribe layer that decouples producers and consumers.

questions.stem: Which TWO statements about BigQuery table optimization are correct? (Select TWO)

options.text: Partitioning can reduce bytes scanned when queries filter the partition column
Clustering can help when queries frequently filter by additional columns
Clustering requires one dataset per table
Partitioning is useful only for unstructured files

questions.explanation: Partitioning and clustering both help optimize scans for common query patterns. The other statements are false.

questions.stem: A team needs governed SQL transformation assets that can be peer-reviewed in source control. Which option aligns BEST?

options.text: Manual edits in the BigQuery console only
A PDF of SQL pasted into email

questions.explanation: Putting Dataform SQL definitions in source control supports review and repeatable deployments.

questions.stem: A job repeatedly fails due to downstream schema drift. What is the BEST platform response?

options.text: Add validation and alerting around the dependency rather than assuming schemas never change
Turn every job into a manual process

questions.explanation: Operational excellence means monitoring dependencies and validating assumptions such as expected schema shape.

questions.stem: A governed analytics platform needs business glossary and metadata discovery rather than only compute. Which service should be part of the design?
questions.explanation: Catalog and governance features belong to Dataplex/Knowledge Catalog, not compute or networking services.

questions.stem: A high-priority ETL reservation is underused overnight but saturated during business hours. What is the BEST interpretation?

options.text: Capacity planning and assignment tuning should be reviewed to match workload patterns
The only solution is to abandon BigQuery
Slot planning has no effect on workload isolation

questions.explanation: Reservation strategy should reflect workload timing and business priorities; imbalance suggests tuning rather than giving up on the model.
